name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 27.04.2026

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 27.04.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 27.04.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on April 24, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 72.30 lakh ha as compared to 70.19 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 30.68 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.
texts.text:  As on April 24, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 72.30 lakh ha as compared to 70.19 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 30.68 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 24.04.2026, 92.62% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over  India. Approximately 99.58%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Gram  (100%),  Urdbean  (99%)  &  Moong  (86%);  99.50%  area  of Oilseeds  majorly  in  Rapeseed  &  Mustard  (100%);  92.43%  area  of  Shri  Anna  cum  Coarse  Cereals majorly in Jowar (96.97%), 47.89% area of Rice and 94.00% area of Wheat has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As on 24.04.2026, 92.62% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over  India. Approximately 99.58%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Gram  (100%),  Urdbean  (99%)  &  Moong  (86%);  99.50%  area  of Oilseeds  majorly  in  Rapeseed  &  Mustard  (100%);  92.43%  area  of  Shri  Anna  cum  Coarse  Cereals majorly in Jowar (96.97%), 47.89% area of Rice and 94.00% area of Wheat has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Northwest  India, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Odisha, Arunachal Pradesh, Konkan-Goa, Coastal Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu.
texts.text: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Northwest  India, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Odisha, Arunachal Pradesh, Konkan-Goa, Coastal Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  April  26  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  3% lower than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 9%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 12%; (iii) Central India: 14%; and South Peninsula: (-) 26%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  April  26  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  3% lower than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 9%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 12%; (iii) Central India: 14%; and South Peninsula: (-) 26%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Fairly  widespread  to  widespread  rainfall  along  with  thunderstorm  &  lightning  and  gusty  winds likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and northeast India during the week.
texts.text:  Fairly  widespread  to  widespread  rainfall  along  with  thunderstorm  &  lightning  and  gusty  winds likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and northeast India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall is also likely over Sub Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and northeast India during the week.
texts.text:  Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall is also likely over Sub Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and northeast India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Thundersquall likely over Assam & Meghalaya during 25 th -27 th April.
texts.text:  Thundersquall likely over Assam & Meghalaya during 25 th -27 th April.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  heavy  rainfall likely  over  Arunachal  Pradesh  on  23 rd &  during  25 th -29 th ;  Assam  & Meghalaya during 25 th -29 th ; Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram & Tripura on 28 th & 29 th ; with isolated very heavy rainfall likely over Arunachal Pradesh during 27 th -29 th ; Assam & Meghalaya during 26 th -29 th April.
texts.text:  Isolated  heavy  rainfall likely  over  Arunachal  Pradesh  on  23 rd &  during  25 th -29 th ;  Assam  & Meghalaya during 25 th -29 th ; Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram & Tripura on 28 th & 29 th ; with isolated very heavy rainfall likely over Arunachal Pradesh during 27 th -29 th ; Assam & Meghalaya during 26 th -29 th April.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated Hailstorm activity likely over Jharkhand on 26 th & 27 th April.
texts.text:  Isolated Hailstorm activity likely over Jharkhand on 26 th & 27 th April.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated heavy rainfall likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim during 25 th -27 th April.
texts.text:  Isolated heavy rainfall likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim during 25 th -27 th April.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall  activity  likely  over  North  India  and  Southern  parts  of  Peninsular India.
texts.text:  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall  activity  likely  over  North  India  and  Southern  parts  of  Peninsular India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat  wave  conditions  likely  to  persist  over  isolated  to  scattered  pockets  of  northwest  &  central India during next 3 days and abate thereafter.
texts.text:  Heat  wave  conditions  likely  to  persist  over  isolated  to  scattered  pockets  of  northwest  &  central India during next 3 days and abate thereafter.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  23  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 74.999 billion cubic meter, which is 40.86% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 114.71 percent of last  year's  storage  and  126.17  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  23  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 74.999 billion cubic meter, which is 40.86% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 114.71 percent of last  year's  storage  and  126.17  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last

texts.label: text
texts.orig: year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During  the  week  (16  April  -  22  April  2026),  lower  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 45%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 15 April 2026) is higher than LPA by 14%.
texts.text:  During  the  week  (16  April  -  22  April  2026),  lower  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 45%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 15 April 2026) is higher than LPA by 14%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over many parts of northeast & adjoining East India, and near normal over some parts of south peninsular India, and below normal/dry weather is likely over the remaining parts of the country.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over many parts of northeast & adjoining East India, and near normal over some parts of south peninsular India, and below normal/dry weather is likely over the remaining parts of the country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: 2. TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (24 April - 30 April 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (24 April - 30 April 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Appreciably above normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over parts of Jammu & Kashmir,  Punjab,  Haryana,  Rajasthan,  Madhya  Pradesh,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Bihar,  Jharkhand,  Odisha, Gangetic West Bengal, Chhattisgarh, Vidarbha, Rayalaseema, Telangana & Kerala; normal to abovenormal maximum temperatures are likely over most parts of the country during the week.
texts.text:  Appreciably above normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over parts of Jammu & Kashmir,  Punjab,  Haryana,  Rajasthan,  Madhya  Pradesh,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Bihar,  Jharkhand,  Odisha, Gangetic West Bengal, Chhattisgarh, Vidarbha, Rayalaseema, Telangana & Kerala; normal to abovenormal maximum temperatures are likely over most parts of the country during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Northwest India till 28th April; gradual fall  by  3-5°C on 29th & 30th April; gradual rise by 2-3°C on 01st & 02nd May and  over Central India till 27th and gradual fall by 2-3°C.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Northwest India till 28th April; gradual fall  by  3-5°C on 29th & 30th April; gradual rise by 2-3°C on 01st & 02nd May and  over Central India till 27th and gradual fall by 2-3°C.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual fall by 2-3°C in maximum temperatures likely over East India during 27thApril -01st May and by 3-4°C in Northeast India during 27th -30th April.
texts.text:  Gradual fall by 2-3°C in maximum temperatures likely over East India during 27thApril -01st May and by 3-4°C in Northeast India during 27th -30th April.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (01  May - 07 May 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (01  May - 07 May 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low  Probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  during  some  days  of  the  week  over  Rajasthan  & adjoining Punjab & Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, & Vidarbha. Appreciably above normal maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of Jammu & Kashmir, and above-normal maximum temperatures (+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over most parts of Northwest & Central India, and many parts of the East & Northeast, and the West coast of India. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely in isolated places over the rest of the country.
texts.text:  Low  Probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  during  some  days  of  the  week  over  Rajasthan  & adjoining Punjab & Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, & Vidarbha. Appreciably above normal maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of Jammu & Kashmir, and above-normal maximum temperatures (+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over most parts of Northwest & Central India, and many parts of the East & Northeast, and the West coast of India. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely in isolated places over the rest of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No warm night conditions are likely during the week.
texts.text:  No warm night conditions are likely during the week.

texts.orig: 3. CROPS

texts.text: Rabi Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rice : About 30.68 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.31 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rice : About 30.68 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.31 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Pulses :  About 17.19 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 15.93 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Pulses :  About 17.19 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 15.93 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Oilseeds :  About 9.21 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 7.71 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Oilseeds :  About 9.21 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 7.71 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. RESERVOIR STORAGE
texts.text: 4. RESERVOIR STORAGE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As on 23 April, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 74.999 BCM (40.86%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
texts.text: As on 23 April, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 74.999 BCM (40.86%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  146 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.
texts.text:  146 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.

texts.orig:  14 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.
texts.text:  14 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.

texts.orig:  Soil moisture during 16 April - 22 April 2026 is similar/better than the average of the past 10 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  Gujarat,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  A.P.,  Karnataka,  Kerala,  Tamil  Nadu, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Uttarakhand, H.P., J & K, Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal and N.E. States.

States.

texts.orig:  It was lower than average of past 10 years in parts of Odisha, Chhattisgarh and Mizoram.
texts.text:  It was lower than average of past 10 years in parts of Odisha, Chhattisgarh and Mizoram.
